signal-accent-future.canonical: to be selected in the next token pass

body.purpose: Default copy, field help, descriptions, and patient-facing instructions.

label.purpose: Buttons, field labels, tabs, compact controls, and navigation items.

mono.purpose: GDT logs, technical traces, rule diagnostics, IDs, and imported file details.

title.purpose: Card titles, table region titles, modal titles, and compact section headers.

shadows.name: hairline-lift
shadows.purpose: Minimal lift for buttons, inputs, and compact surfaces that need tactile feedback.

shadows.name: panel-lift
shadows.purpose: Menus, popovers, dialogs, and surfaces that must sit over the task.

shadows.name: strong-overlay-lift
shadows.purpose: Rare overlay or drag state only.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default product UI feedback for hover, focus, active, and open states.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Disable choreography; preserve instant state feedback.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary action button for booking progress, saves, and confirmed operational commands.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 8px 16px; border: 0; border-radius: 6px; background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-foreground); font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25; white-space: nowrap; box-shadow: var(--shadow-xs); transition: background-color 180ms ease-out, box-shadow 180ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--primary) 90%, transparent); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); } .ds-btn-primary:active { box-shadow: none; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Quiet supporting action for alternate branches and lower-priority commands.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 8px 16px; border: 0; border-radius: 6px; background: var(--secondary); color: var(--secondary-foreground); font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25; white-space: nowrap; box-shadow: var(--shadow-xs); transition: background-color 180ms ease-out, box-shadow 180ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--secondary) 80%, var(--foreground)); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Bordered action for toolbar commands and choices that need structure without primary emphasis.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 6px; background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25; white-space: nowrap; box-shadow: var(--shadow-xs); transition: background-color 180ms ease-out, color 180ms ease-out, box-shadow 180ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: var(--accent); color: var(--accent-foreground); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Default field treatment for patient intake, filters, and operational forms.
components.css: .ds-field { display: grid; gap: 6px; color: var(--foreground); font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; } .ds-input { height: 36px; width: 100%; min-width: 0; border: 1px solid var(--input); border-radius: 6px; background: transparent; color: var(--foreground); padding: 4px 12px; font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.5; box-shadow: var(--shadow-xs); transition: border-color 180ms ease-out, box-shadow 180ms ease-out; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); }

components.name: Workbench Card
components.description: Structured product container for dashboard entries, rule modules, and patient booking panels.
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 24px; border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 12px; background: var(--card); color: var(--card-foreground); padding: 24px; box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm); font-family: var(--font-sans); } .ds-card h3 { margin: 0; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1; } .ds-card p { margin: 0; color: var(--muted-foreground); font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.5; } .ds-card:focus-within { box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring) 30%, transparent); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Compact status or metadata marker. Use strong fill only when status matters.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 4px; width: fit-content; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 6px; background: var(--primary); color: var(--primary-foreground); padding: 2px 8px; font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-badge:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); }

components.name: Segmented Tabs
components.description: Compact local navigation for product modes and workflow branches.
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0; height: 36px; width: fit-content; border-radius: 8px; background: var(--muted); color: var(--muted-foreground); padding: 3px; font-family: var(--font-sans); } .ds-tab { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 29px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 6px; background: transparent; color: var(--foreground); padding: 4px 8px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; transition: background-color 180ms ease-out, color 180ms ease-out, box-shadow 180ms ease-out; } .ds-tab-active { background: var(--background); box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm); } .ds-tab:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: var(--ring); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in oklch, var(--ring) 50%, transparent); }

narrative.overview:
Praxisplaner is a clinical product interface for high-trust scheduling work. The system should feel like a precise operations desk: clear surfaces, low visual noise, direct state feedback, and no decorative delay between intent and action.

The current implementation is built on shadcn New York primitives with Geist, OKLCH neutral tokens, low-radius controls, compact heights, and a restrained black-and-white base. Future visual work should keep that operational density but move away from generic monochrome SaaS minimalism through sharper corners, stronger hierarchy, and one disciplined poppy accent for signal.

This system rejects generic Vercel-style template polish, over-rounded cards, pill-heavy layouts, decorative gradients, soft ghost-card shadows, playful medical illustrations, and marketing-page composition inside task surfaces.
keyCharacteristics: Product-first density for staff workflows and calm linearity for patient booking.
Neutral clinical base with exact contrast, stable borders, and clear focus rings.
Poppy accent used as signal, not decoration.
Low-radius, precise controls rather than bubbly SaaS softness.
German healthcare copy that names the object and the consequence.

rules.body: Poppy accent color must stay rare. If it appears on more than roughly 10% of a product screen, it is no longer signal.
rules.name: The Signal Rarity Rule

rules.body: Body text, placeholders, labels, and disabled-adjacent explanations must remain readable against their actual surface. Do not soften text into pale gray for elegance.
rules.name: The Clinical Contrast Rule

rules.body: Do not introduce display fonts into labels, buttons, tables, forms, or calendar cells. Product UI uses one sans family well.
rules.name: The Product Type Rule

rules.body: Use fixed rem sizes for product UI. Fluid clamp scales belong to marketing surfaces, not dense scheduling tools.
rules.name: The No Fluid UI Type Rule

rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. If a shadow is visible from across the room, it is too decorative for this product.
rules.name: The Flat Workbench Rule

rules.body: Never combine a 1px border with a wide decorative drop shadow. Pick border structure or compact lift, not both.
rules.name: The No Ghost Card Rule

dos: Do keep the default register product-first: dense, calm, and task-oriented.
Do use the future poppy accent only for primary action, selection, current context, and meaningful status.
Do keep corners sharp and intentional: 6px for controls, 8-12px for larger containers, never bubbly by default.
Do preserve strong keyboard focus and WCAG 2.2 AA contrast across booking and staff workflows.
Do use borders, tonal panels, and sticky structure to explain scheduling logic before adding visual effects.
donts: Don't ship generic monochrome SaaS minimalism that feels like a default Vercel template with no healthcare-specific judgment.
Don't use over-rounded cards, pill-heavy layouts, decorative gradients, or soft ghost-card shadows.
Don't add playful medical illustrations or marketing-page composition inside task surfaces.
Don't make patients feel they are operating internal practice software.
Don't use side-stripe borders greater than 1px as accent decoration.
Don't use gradient text, glassmorphism as default, or decorative page-load choreography.
